hook: Moscow Times and The Guardian World — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Russia-Ukraine war. Nobody else did.

The paradox: Russia's independent (Latvia-based exile) Moscow Times and the UK Guardian both foregrounded the mass deportation of Ukrainian children as a live atrocity in the same news cycle — one framing it through British sanctions, the other through an EU art installation — p…

Silence as data: `france` didn't cover this — they covered No dedicated French bucket. France is an active EU party in Ukraine peace discussions and one of Ukraine's major military backers, but French coverage is absent from this corpus. instead.

**Globe and Mail** (canada): Immersive account of NATO wargames in Latvia with Sgt. Cody Baltzer — shows how Ukraine's drone doctrine is being actively institutionalised across NATO before any great-power engagement.
